ToolUseBlock(id='toolu_01VUXdg11ryKLpJF37E1KLj9', name='Write', input={'file_path': '/workspace/start.sh', 'content': '#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Anomali Agentic SOC Platform — self-contained dev server bootstrap.
# Runs in a clean container: installs Node.js (if missing), installs npm deps, starts Vite on port 3000.

set -e

APP_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)/app"
cd "$APP_DIR"

echo "=========================================="
echo "  Anomali Agentic SOC Platform - Bootstrap"
echo "=========================================="
echo "App directory: $APP_DIR"

# ---------- Node.js ----------
need_node=true
if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  node_major=$(node -p "process.versions.node.split('.')[0]" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if [ "$node_major" -ge 18 ]; then
    need_node=false
  fi
fi

if [ "$need_node" = true ]; then
  echo "[1/3] Installing Node.js 20 (LTS)..."
  if command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
    apt-get update -y
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl ca-certificates gnupg
    curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs
  elif command -v yum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    curl -fsSL https://rpm.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -
    yum install -y nodejs
  elif command -v apk >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    apk add --no-cache nodejs npm
  else
    echo "ERROR: Unable to install Node.js automatically. Please install Node.js >=18 manually." >&2
    exit 1
  fi
else
  echo "[1/3] Node.js $(node -v) detected — skipping install."
fi

echo "    Node:  $(node -v)"
echo "    NPM:   $(npm -v)"

# ---------- Dependencies ----------
echo "[2/3] Installing npm dependencies..."
if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
  npm ci --no-audit --no-fund || npm install --no-audit --no-fund
else
  npm install --no-audit --no-fund
fi

# ---------- Start ----------
echo "[3/3] Starting Vite dev server on http://localhost:3000"
echo "      (Press Ctrl+C to stop.)"
echo ""
exec npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 3000
'})

A polished, multi-page marketing front-end that reproduces the Anomali Agentic SOC Platform website. The site introduces the unified Agentic SOC Platform (Unified Security Data Lake · ThreatStream Next-Gen · Agentic AI), a Resource Center with filters/search/pagination, and shared navigation/footer across all pages.

## Project Overview

This single-page application (SPA) delivers:

- A **Homepage** with hero, three platform-layer cards, partner trust strip, outcome stats, and CTA band.
- A dedicated **Agentic SOC Platform** product page covering the four SOC stages (Security, Investigation, Remediation, Intelligence) and core capabilities.
- Three deep-dive product pages: **Unified Security Data Lake**, **ThreatStream Next-Gen**, and **Agentic AI Capabilities**.
- A comprehensive **Resource Center** with filter pills, search, grid layout, pagination, "Read More" actions, and empty/loading states.
- Consistent **top navigation** (Products · Use Cases · Marketplace · Resources · Company · Partners) and a comprehensive **footer** across every page.
- Responsive design (desktop / tablet / mobile), animated hover states, a sticky header, and CTA bands with **green "Schedule a Demo" / "Talk to Sales"** buttons throughout.

## Technology Stack

| Layer        | Choice                          | Why                                                     |
| ------------ | ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Framework    | **React 19**                    | Component model, ecosystem, mature router               |
| Routing      | **react-router-dom 7**          | Client-side routing across 9 pages                       |
| Build tool   | **Vite 7**                      | Fast HMR, zero-config dev server on port 3000           |
| Styling      | **Hand-crafted CSS (variables)**| Predictable, no runtime cost, design-system tokens      |
| Fonts        | **Inter** (local woff)          | Provided in `/workspace/resources/fonts`                |
| Assets       | Local images from `/resources`  | Real Anomali marketing thumbnails / logos               |

## 1. Application Architecture

### 1.1 High-level
A **React SPA** with **client-side routing** (react-router-dom) sitting on top of **Vite**. The application is a static, marketing-style site: there is no API. All page content is rendered from in-repo data and JSX. State is local (`useState`/`useMemo`) — no global store is needed.

### 1.2 Component Hierarchy
```
<BrowserRouter>
  <App>
    <ScrollToTop/>
    <Header/>
      └─ NavLinks (6) + brand + Sign-in/Schedule-a-Demo CTAs + mobile toggle
    <main>
      <Routes>
        / ............................. <Home/>
        /products ..................... <AgenticSOC/>
        /products/agentic-soc-platform  <AgenticSOC/>
        /products/unified-security-data-lake  <DataLake/>
        /products/threatstream ........ <ThreatStream/>
        /products/agentic-ai .......... <AgenticAI/>
        /resources .................... <Resources/>
        /use-cases /marketplace /company /partners  <Generic/>
        *  ............................ <Generic 404/>
      </Routes>
    </main>
    <Footer/>
      └─ 4 link columns + brand panel + Talk-to-Sales CTA + contact
  </App>
</BrowserRouter>
```

### 1.3 Routing Plan
- All routes are declared in `App.jsx`.
- Header `NavLink`s automatically highlight the active route.
- `ScrollToTop` listens to `useLocation()` and resets scroll on every navigation.
- A wildcard route renders a friendly 404 via `<Generic/>`.

### 1.4 Shared building blocks
- **PlatformLayers** — reusable 3-card grid (used on Home and Agentic SOC pages). Each card is a `<Link>` to its dedicated page, satisfying both "click the layer card on Home" and "click POWERED BY layer cards on Agentic SOC page" requirements with a single source of truth.
- **CTABand** — green "Schedule a Demo" / "Talk to Sales" banner reused on every page.
- **Header / Footer** — consistent across every route.

## 2. Technology Stack

| Concern             | Choice                              |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| UI framework        | React 19                            |
| Router              | react-router-dom 7                  |
| Dev server / bundler| Vite 7 (`@vitejs/plugin-react`)     |
| Language            | Modern JavaScript (ESM, JSX)        |
| Styling             | Hand-authored CSS with custom props |
| State               | Local component state               |
| Fonts               | Inter (woff, served locally)        |
| Asset pipeline      | Vite static serving from `/public`  |

### Why not Tailwind / a UI library?
The site requires a distinct, branded dark visual identity (navy + signature green) and a custom layer-diagram component. Hand-authored CSS with a small token system keeps the bundle minimal, eliminates a build step, and produces a more polished result than a generic component library would.

## 3. Project Directory Structure

```
/workspace
├── start.sh                  # bootstrap
├── README.md
├── DESIGN.md
├── prompt.txt
├── resources/                # source assets (untouched)
└── app/
    ├── index.html
    ├── package.json
    ├── vite.config.js
    ├── public/assets/
    │   ├── fonts/            # Inter woff files
    │   └── images/           # All marketing images
    └── src/
        ├── main.jsx          # entry
        ├── App.jsx           # routes
        ├── styles/global.css # design system + components
        ├── components/       # Header, Footer, CTABand, PlatformLayers
        ├── data/resources.js # mock catalog for Resource Center
        └── pages/            # one .jsx per route family
```

## 4. Design System

### 4.1 Color tokens (`global.css`)
| Token             | Value      | Role                            |
| ----------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------- |
| `--bg-deep`       | `#060d1e`  | Page background                 |
| `--bg-dark`       | `#0a1430`  | Section panel                   |
| `--bg-card`       | `#131f44`  | Card surface                    |
| `--accent`        | `#00e08a`  | Primary CTA + active state      |
| `--accent-2`      | `#4f8cff`  | Secondary accent (layers, hero) |
| `--accent-3`      | `#b388ff`  | Tertiary accent                 |
| `--text` / muted  | `#e6ecff` / `#98a2c2` | Body type            |

### 4.2 Typography
- **Inter** loaded locally via `@font-face`.
- Fluid type scale: `h1` clamps 34→60px, `h2` clamps 28→44px.
- Eyebrow style (`.eyebrow`): uppercase, 0.18em tracking, green.

### 4.3 Components / patterns
- **`.btn`** — pill button with `.btn-primary` (green), `.btn-outline`, `.btn-ghost`, `.btn-link` (with arrow on hover).
- **`.card`** — gradient overlay, hover lift, used everywhere.
- **`.layer-diagram`** — visual "row" stack for platform layers; active row uses the green accent and a glowing dot.
- **`.stages`** — 4-column numbered process strip for the Agentic SOC page.
- **`.stat`** — large green numeric tiles for performance metrics.
- **`.video-card`** — 16:9 thumbnail with a circular green play overlay.
- **`.resource-card`** — Resource Center grid card.
- **`.pagination`**, **`.pill`** — Resource Center controls.
- **`.cta-band`** — reusable banner.
- **`.logo-strip`** — partner / customer logos with greyscale → color hover.

### 4.4 Responsive strategy
- Mobile-first feels (single column under 720px, drawer nav), but visual design is desktop-led.
- Three breakpoints: `> 1024px` (3+ column grids), `720–1024px` (2 columns), `<= 720px` (1 column + hamburger).

## 5. Implementation Details

### 5.1 Homepage
- Hero uses two backgrounds: radial accent gradients + a faint grid mask for a "data center" feel.
- Right-hand hero card shows all three platform layers active, signalling the unified message.
- Partner logo strip uses CSS filter for a uniform muted look.

### 5.2 Platform pages
- Each layer page has its own value proposition, 3 capability cards, ~4 detailed capabilities, a performance stat row, and use-case grid with "Read More" buttons (per prompt).
- Cross-links: each page links back to the others via the navigation and bottom CTAs.

### 5.3 Resource Center
- Data lives in `src/data/resources.js`: **39 resources** spanning the **9 categories** in the prompt (Solution Brief, White Paper, eBook, Guide, Datasheet, Product, Use Case, Video, Webinar) plus an "All" filter.
- Filtering: simple `filter()` on the active type pill.
- Search: case-insensitive substring match across title, description and type.
- Both filter and search reset to page 1 to keep the URL of UX coherent.
- Pagination: 9 cards per page, prev/next + numbered buttons; "Showing X–Y of N" counter; empty-state card when nothing matches; "Clear filters" affordance.

### 5.4 Generic pages
- `/use-cases`, `/marketplace`, `/company`, `/partners` reuse a single `Generic` component, parameterized with title + subtitle. Each ends in a CTA band — satisfying "every page includes Schedule a Demo or similar CTA in green".

### 5.5 Accessibility
- Semantic landmarks: `<header>`, `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<footer>`, `<article>`.
- All images have `alt`. Buttons have `aria-label`s where they are icon-only.
- Sufficient contrast ratios for text on dark backgrounds.

### 5.6 Performance
- All assets are served as static files. Images use `loading="lazy"` in the Resource Center.
- No client-side data fetching → first interactive paint depends only on JS bundle size.
- Hover transitions are 200–250ms and `transform`/`opacity`-only to avoid layout thrashing.

## 6. Deployment

The repository is fully reproducible from `/workspace/start.sh`:

1. Detects whether Node.js ≥18 is already installed.
2. If not, installs Node.js 20 LTS via the system package manager (apt / yum / apk).
3. Installs npm dependencies with `npm ci`, falling back to `npm install`.
4. Starts `vite` bound to `0.0.0.0:3000`.

Acceptance: after `bash /workspace/start.sh`, the site is fully operational at **http://localhost:3000** with every feature listed in the prompt working.
